wooper-paldea: After losing a territorial struggle, Wooper began living on land. The Pokémon changed over time, developing a poisonous film to protect its body.
charizard-mega-x: The overwhelming power that fills its entire body causes it to turn black and creates intense blue flames.
charizard-mega-y: Its bond with its Trainer is the source of its power. It boasts speed and maneuverability greater than that of a jet.
charizard-gmax: This colossal, flame-winged figure of a Charizard was brought about by Gigantamax energy.
raichu-alola: It's believed that the weather, climate, and food of the Alola region all play a part in causing Pikachu to evolve into this form of Raichu.
raichu-mega-x: It resembles an X as it flies through the air with 50 million volts of electricity sparking from its ears and forked tail.
raichu-mega-y: It fires bolts of electricity from the tip of its tail and from the spiky tufts of fur growing out of its temples. This electricity forms the letter Y.
mewtwo-mega-x: Psychic power has augmented its muscles. It has a grip strength of one ton and can sprint 100 meters in two seconds flat!
mewtwo-mega-y: Despite its diminished size, its mental power has grown phenomenally. With a mere thought, it can smash a skyscraper to smithereens.
meowth-alola: It's accustomed to luxury because it used to live with Alolan royalty. As a result, it's very picky about food.
meowth-galar: Living with a savage, seafaring people has toughened this Pokémon's body so much that parts of it have turned to iron.
meowth-gmax: The pattern that has appeared on its giant coin is thought to be the key to unlocking the secrets of the Dynamax phenomenon.
venusaur-mega: In order to support its flower, which has grown larger due to Mega Evolution, its back and legs have become stronger.
venusaur-gmax: In battle, this Pokémon swings around two thick vines. If these vines slammed into a 10-story building, they could easily topple it.
blastoise-mega: The cannon on its back is as powerful as a tank gun. Its tough legs and back enable it to withstand the recoil from firing the cannon.
blastoise-gmax: It's not very good at precision shooting. When attacking, it just fires its 31 cannons over and over and over.
butterfree-gmax: Crystallized Gigantamax energy makes up this Pokémon's blindingly bright and highly toxic scales.
beedrill-mega: Its legs have become poison stingers. It stabs its prey repeatedly with the stingers on its limbs, dealing the final blow with the stinger on its rear.
pidgeot-mega: With its muscular strength now greatly increased, it can fly continuously for two weeks without resting.
rattata-alola: Night after night, they sneak into people's homes seeking food. A massive outbreak of them has become an issue of public concern.
sandshrew-alola: It lives on snowy mountains. Its steel shell is very hard—so much so, it can’t roll its body up into a ball.
sandslash-alola: This Pokémon’s steel spikes are sheathed in ice. Stabs from these spikes cause deep wounds and severe frostbite as well.
clefable-mega: It flies by using the power of moonlight to control gravity within a radius of around it.
vulpix-alola: They live together in a skulk, helping one another. Before eating their prey, they freeze it solid with their −58 degree Fahrenheit breath.
ninetales-alola: Possessing a calm demeanor, this Pokémon was revered as a deity incarnate before it was identified as a regional variant of Ninetales.
diglett-alola: The things growing from its head are whiskers that have become metallic. Diglett communicates with its comrades by waving them.
dugtrio-alola: Its shining gold whiskers are advanced sensors that can detect vibrations from sounds several miles away.
persian-alola: The round face of Alolan Persian is considered to be a symbol of prosperity in the Alola region, so these Pokémon are very well cared for.
growlithe-hisui: They patrol their territory in pairs. I believe the igneous rock components in the fur of this species are the result of volcanic activity in its habitat.
arcanine-hisui: Snaps at its foes with fangs cloaked in blazing flame. Despite its bulk, it deftly feints every which way, leading opponents on a deceptively merry chase as it all but dances around them.
alakazam-mega: Alakazam has traded away its muscles, unleashing its true power. With its psychic powers, it can foresee all things.
machamp-gmax: The Gigantamax energy coursing through its arms makes its punches hit as hard as bomb blasts.
victreebel-mega: The volume of this Pokémon's acid has increased due to Mega Evolution, filling its mouth. If it's not careful, the acid will overflow and spill out.
geodude-alola: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attaches firmly to the portions of its body that are particularly magnetic.
graveler-alola: They eat rocks and often get into a scrap over them. The impact of Graveler smashing together causes a flash of light and a booming noise.
golem-alola: It’s grumpy and stubborn. If you upset it, it discharges electricity from the surface of its body and growls with a voice like thunder.
ponyta-galar: Its small horn hides a healing power. With a few rubs from this Pokémon's horn, any slight wound you have will be healed.
rapidash-galar: Little can stand up to its . Unleashed from this Pokémon's horn, the move will punch a hole right through a thick metal sheet.
slowpoke-galar: It gives off a stimulating scent because of chemicals that have seeped into its body from the spice that it eats as its staple food.
slowbro-mega: When bathed in the energy of Mega Evolution, Shellder converts into impregnable armor. There is virtually no change in Slowpoke.
slowbro-galar: Shellder seems blissful over the taste of Slowbro even though the toxins seeping from the bite have caused Shellder to faint.
farfetchd-galar: The Farfetch'd of the Galar region are brave warriors, and they wield thick, tough leeks in battle.
grimer-alola: They store their toxins inside their bodies, so unlike Grimer from other regions, they don’t smell bad even up close.
muk-alola: There are over a hundred kinds of poison inside its body. Chemical reactions between different poisons are the source of its vitality.
gengar-mega: It can pass through other dimensions and appear anywhere. It caused a stir one time when it stuck just one leg out of a wall.
gengar-gmax: Rumor has it that its gigantic mouth leads not into its body, filled with cursed energy, but instead directly to the afterlife.
kingler-gmax: The flow of Gigantamax energy has spurred this Pokémon's left pincer to grow to an enormous size. That claw can pulverize anything.
voltorb-hisui: An enigmatic Pokémon that happens to bear a resemblance to a Poké Ball. When excited, it discharges the electric current it has stored in its belly, then lets out a great, uproarious laugh.
electrode-hisui: The tissue on the surface of its body is curiously similar in composition to an Apricorn. When irritated, this Pokémon lets loose an electric current equal to 20 lightning bolts.
exeggutor-alola: It swings its long neck like a whip and smacks its head into opponents. This makes Exeggutor itself a little dizzy too.
marowak-alola: This Pokémon sets the bone it holds on fire and dances through the night as a way to mourn its fallen allies.
weezing-galar: This Pokémon consumes particles that contaminate the air. Instead of leaving droppings, it expels clean air.
kangaskhan-mega: Its child has grown rapidly, thanks to the energy of Mega Evolution. Mother and child show off their harmonious teamwork in battle.
starmie-mega: Its movements have become more humanlike. Whether it's simply trying to communicate or wants to supplant humanity is unclear.
pinsir-mega: After Mega Evolution, it becomes able to fly. Perhaps because it's so happy, it rarely touches the ground.
gyarados-mega: It jets water from the orifices on its sides, streaking above the water surface at supersonic speed.
lapras-gmax: Over 5,000 people can ride on its shell at once. And it's a very comfortable ride, without the slightest shaking or swaying.
eevee-gmax: Gigantamax energy upped the fluffiness of the fur around Eevee's neck. The fur will envelop a foe, capturing its body and captivating its mind.
aerodactyl-mega: Parts of its body have become stone. Some scholars claim that this is Aerodactyl's true appearance.
snorlax-gmax: Gigantamax energy has affected stray seeds and even pebbles that got stuck to Snorlax, making them grow to a huge size.
articuno-galar: Its feather-like blades are composed of psychic energy and can shear through thick iron sheets as if they were paper.
zapdos-galar: When its feathers rub together, they produce a crackling sound like the zapping of electricity. That's why this Pokémon is called Zapdos.
moltres-galar: This Pokémon's sinister, flame-like aura will consume the spirit of any creature it hits. Victims become burned-out shadows of themselves.
dragonite-mega: Mega Evolution has excessively powered up this Pokémon's feelings of kindness. It finishes off its opponents with mercy in its heart.
meganium-mega: This Pokémon can fire a tremendously powerful from its four flowers. Another name for this is Cannon.
typhlosion-hisui: Said to purify lost, forsaken souls with its flames and guide them to the afterlife. I believe its form has been influenced by the energy of the sacred mountain towering at Hisui's center.
feraligatr-mega: With its arms and hoodlike fin, this Pokémon forms a gigantic set of jaws with a bite 10 times as powerful as Mega Feraligatr's actual jaws.
ampharos-mega: Massive amounts of energy intensely stimulated Ampharos's cells, apparently awakening its long-sleeping dragon's blood.
slowking-galar: It administers its potions to weakened Pokémon it sees. These potions are derived from poison and secreted from holes in Slowking’s horns.
steelix-mega: To protect itself from opponents' attacks, it uses magnetism to control pieces of its hard outer shell that have flaked off.
qwilfish-hisui: Fishers detest this troublesome Pokémon because it sprays poison from its spines, getting it everywhere. A different form of Qwilfish lives in other regions.
scizor-mega: Due to the effects of Mega Evolution, its pincers have taken on a more diabolical form, ripping anything they pinch to shreds.
heracross-mega: It can grip things with its two horns and lift 500 times its own body weight.
sneasel-hisui: Its sturdy, curved claws are ideal for traversing precipitous cliffs. From the tips of these claws drips a venom that infiltrates the nerves of any prey caught in Sneasel's grasp.
corsola-galar: Watch your step when wandering areas oceans once covered. What looks like a stone could be this Pokémon, and it will curse you if you kick it.
skarmory-mega: It flies faster than the speed of sound. After whipping up shock waves to send enemies flying, it finishes them off with its talons.
houndoom-mega: Houndoom's entire body generates heat when it Mega Evolves. Its intense, fiery breath turns its opponents to charcoal.
tyranitar-mega: The effects of Mega Evolution make it more ferocious than ever. It's unclear whether it can even hear its Trainer's orders.
sceptile-mega: The seeds on its back are filled with energy from Mega Evolution. The Pokémon detonates these seeds to launch the tip of its tail.
blaziken-mega: Boiling-hot blood courses through its entire body, doubling the sharpness of its moves. It subdues opponents in the blink of an eye.
swampert-mega: The water within its body further strengthens its already enlarged muscles, using the same principles as a hydraulic pump.
zigzagoon-galar: Its restlessness has it constantly running around. If it sees another Pokémon, it will purposely run into them in order to start a fight.
linoone-galar: It uses its long tongue to taunt opponents. Once the opposition is enraged, this Pokémon hurls itself at the opponent, tackling them forcefully.
gardevoir-mega: When it opens the red plate on its chest and unleashes its heart, its strongest psychic power is released.
sableye-mega: The jewel from its chest, which has grown gigantic due to the effects of Mega Evolution, can turn back any attack.
mawile-mega: Its two sets of jaws thrash about violently as if they each had a will of their own. One gnash from them can turn a boulder to dust.
aggron-mega: Energy from Mega Evolution has turned the iron inside this Pokémon into steel armor that covers Mega Aggron's whole body.
medicham-mega: Thanks to Mega Evolution, its mental power is limitless. It conjures up arms imbued with malice to defeat its enemies.
manectric-mega: Mega Evolution fills its body with a tremendous amount of electricity, but it's too much for Manectric to fully control.
sharpedo-mega: The spines sprouting from its head are transformed fangs. If they're injured or broken off, the spines will regenerate countless times.
camerupt-mega: When this Pokémon's rage reaches a boiling point, the huge volcano in the hump on its back erupts violently, spewing molten lava.
altaria-mega: Its down is so soft, it seems as if a touch could melt it. But it's strong enough that just a few strands could be used to hoist a dump truck.
banette-mega: Mega Evolution increases its vindictiveness, and the cursing power that was held back by its zipper comes spilling out.
chimecho-mega: It vibrates its entire body to attack in all directions using sound waves. Any who hear the vibrations get so confused, they forget who they are.
absol-mega: When this Pokémon whips the winglike fur on its back as though beating its wings, it sends an intimidating aura flying at its opponents.
absol-mega-z: Using fur that it has made into sharp, clawlike shapes, it cuts down foes with a single blow. This is an act of kindness to keep them from suffering.
glalie-mega: When it spews stupendously cold air from its broken mouth, the entire area around it gets whited out.
salamence-mega: To streamline itself for flight, it pulls its forelegs into its shell. Salamence flies at high speeds even when faced with complex topographical features.
metagross-mega: This form results from one Metagross, one Metang, and two Beldum linking up.
latias-mega: Both its flying capabilities and its attack power have increased. It scatters transparent down to confuse its enemies.
latios-mega: It flies at speeds exceeding Mach 4. It is among the top tier of all Pokémon in terms of aerial combat capability.
kyogre-primal: Kyogre reverted to this state via the Blue Orb, which contains the energy of the primordial world. Kyogre's body resembles seawater.
groudon-primal: This is Groudon's true form, restored using the Red Orb. Energy spills from its body in the form of magma.
rayquaza-mega: A shooting star streaking across the night sky may, in fact, be Mega Rayquaza flying gracefully after a meteoroid.
staraptor-mega: Mega Staraptor is a top-class flier. It can easily soar through the sky while gripping a Steelix that weighs more than .
lopunny-mega: It swings its ears like whips and strikes its enemies with them. It has an intensely combative disposition.
garchomp-mega: Its disposition is more vicious than before its Mega Evolution. Garchomp carves its opponents up with the scythes on both arms.
garchomp-mega-z: Garchomp has gained a new Mega-Evolved form. It flies around foes at Mach speed and cuts them to shreds with its sinister wing claws.
lucario-mega: Black streaks all over its body show where its auras and the energy of Mega Evolution intermingled and raced through it.
lucario-mega-z: By completely cloaking itself in its aura, Mega Lucario Z can parry all manner of attacks, battling as if it were gracefully dancing.
abomasnow-mega: The buds on its back have sprouted into impressive icicles that can whip up massive blizzards of −22 degrees Fahrenheit.
gallade-mega: It gets power from the bond it has with its Trainer. Mega Evolution has given it plates to slice its enemies and a cape to protect its body.
froslass-mega: This Pokémon can use eerie cold air imbued with ghost energy to freeze even insubstantial things, such as flames or the wind.
heatran-mega: It's said that if it goes all out, it can heat its body up to temperatures over 1.8 million degrees Fahrenheit. This heat keeps enemies at bay.
darkrai-mega: Its dark power blocks out the sun, plunging the surrounding area into darkness. There is no escaping its evil eye.
emboar-mega: Brandishing a blazing flame shaped like a serpentine spear, it rushes in to rescue its imperiled allies.
samurott-hisui: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.
excadrill-mega: If this Pokémon brings its arms and head together to form a streamlined shape and spins at high speeds, it can destroy anything.
audino-mega: Anyone who touches the secondary feelers that have sprouted from the base of its throat will fall into a deep sleep.
scolipede-mega: Its deadly venom gives off a faint glow. The venom affects Scolipede's mind, honing its viciousness.
lilligant-hisui: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.
darumaka-galar: It lived in snowy areas for so long that its fire sac cooled off and atrophied. It now has an organ that generates cold instead.
scrafty-mega: Mega Evolution has caused Scrafty's shed skin to turn white, growing tough and supple. Of course, this Pokémon is still as feisty as ever.
yamask-galar: A clay slab with cursed engravings took possession of a Yamask. The slab is said to be absorbing the Yamask's dark power.
garbodor-gmax: Due to Gigantamax energy, this Pokémon's toxic gas has become much thicker, congealing into masses shaped like discarded toys.
zorua-hisui: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.
zoroark-hisui: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.
eelektross-mega: It now generates 10 times the electricity it did before Mega Evolving. It discharges this electricity from its false Eelektrik, which are made of mucus.
chandelure-mega: One of its eyes is a window linking our world with the afterlife. This Pokémon draws in hatred and converts it into power.
stunfisk-galar: Living in mud with a high iron content has given it a strong steel body.
golurk-mega: The energy within Golurk has been stimulated by Mega Evolution. The Pokémon could explode at any moment.
braviary-hisui: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.
chesnaught-mega: It has fortified armor and a will to defend at all costs. Both are absurdly strong.
delphox-mega: It wields flaming branches to dazzle its opponents before incinerating them with a huge fireball.
greninja-mega: This Pokémon spins a giant shuriken at high speed to make it float, then clings to it upside down to catch opponents unawares.
pyroar-mega: This Pokémon spews flames hotter than . It swings around its grand, blazing mane as it protects its allies.
floette-mega: The Eternal Flower has absorbed all the energy from Mega Evolution. The flower now attacks enemies on its own.
meowstic-female: Female Meowstic are more aggressive than males. Their expression remains blank even while they're wielding immense psychic power.
meowstic-mega: Mega Meowstic can use its psychic power to compress or expand anything. It overwhelms foes by contorting space itself.
malamar-mega: It uses its colorful lights to overwrite the personalities and memories of others—and to control them.
barbaracle-mega: It uses its many arms to toy with its opponents. This keeps the head extremely busy.
dragalge-mega: It spits a liquid that causes the regenerative power of cells to run wild. The liquid is deadly poison to everything other than itself.
hawlucha-mega: Mega Evolution has pumped up all its muscles. Hawlucha flexes to show off its strength.
sliggoo-hisui: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
goodra-hisui: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
avalugg-hisui: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
zygarde-mega: In response to people's emotions during an unprecedented crisis, Zygarde Mega Evolves and calms the situation with its unmatched power.
diancie-mega: This Pokémon is also known as the Royal Pink Princess. The dazzling, sparkling diamond on its forehead is a whopping 2,000 carats.
decidueye-hisui: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.
crabominable-mega: It can pulverize reinforced concrete with a light swing of one of its fists, each of which is covered in a thick layer of ice.
golisopod-mega: It uses four of its arms to fiercely assail its foes. Once they've been pushed to the brink of defeat, it finishes them off with the arms it kept hidden.
drampa-mega: Drampa's cells have been invigorated, allowing it to regain its youth. It manipulates the atmosphere to summon storms.
magearna-mega: This is Magearna once a previously hidden mode activates. The emotions Magearna had begun to feel now hide away as it fells foe after foe.
zeraora-mega: It stores up 10 lightning strikes' worth of electricity. When it stops limiting itself, it's in the strongest class of electric Pokémon.
melmetal-gmax: In a distant land, there are legends about a cyclopean giant. In fact, the giant was a Melmetal that was flooded with Gigantamax energy.
rillaboom-gmax: Gigantamax energy has caused Rillaboom's stump to grow into a drum set that resembles a forest.
cinderace-gmax: Infused with Cinderace's fighting spirit, the never misses its targets and completely roasts opponents.
inteleon-gmax: Gigantamax Inteleon's Water Gun move fires at Mach 7. As the Pokémon takes aim, it uses the crest on its head to gauge wind and temperature.
corviknight-gmax: Imbued with Gigantamax energy, its wings can whip up winds more forceful than any a hurricane could muster. The gusts blow everything away.
orbeetle-gmax: Its brain has grown to a gargantuan size, as has the rest of its body. This Pokémon's intellect and psychic abilities are overpowering.
drednaw-gmax: It responded to Gigantamax energy by becoming bipedal. First it comes crashing down on foes, and then it finishes them off with its massive jaws.
coalossal-gmax: Its body is a colossal stove. With Gigantamax energy stoking the fire, this Pokémon's flame burns hotter than .
flapple-gmax: Under the influence of Gigantamax energy, it produces much more sweet nectar, and its shape has changed to resemble a giant apple.
appletun-gmax: It blasts its opponents with massive amounts of sweet, sticky nectar, drowning them under the deluge.
sandaconda-gmax: Its sand pouch has grown to tremendous proportions. More than 1,000,000 tons of sand now swirl around its body.
centiskorch-gmax: Gigantamax energy has evoked a rise in its body temperature, now reaching over . Its heat waves incinerate its enemies.
hatterene-gmax: This Pokémon can read the emotions of creatures over away. The minute it senses hostility, it goes on the attack.
grimmsnarl-gmax: By transforming its leg hair, this Pokémon delivers power-packed drill kicks that can bore huge holes in Galar's terrain.
alcremie-gmax: Cream pours endlessly from this Pokémon's body. The cream stiffens when compressed by an impact. A harder impact results in harder cream.
falinks-mega: Mega Falinks has taken on the ultimate battle formation, which can be achieved only if the troopers and brass have the strongest of bonds.
indeedee-female: It dislikes conflict. If a fight occurs in its territory, it uses psychic power to throw things at the combatants in an effort to drive them away.
copperajah-gmax: So much power is packed within its trunk that if it were to unleash that power, the resulting blast could level mountains and change the landscape.
duraludon-gmax: It's grown to resemble a skyscraper. Parts of its towering body glow due to a profusion of energy.
zarude-dada: This Zarude left its pack to raise . The cloth Zarude would wrap the child with has a comforting scent that calmed the child's crying.
ursaluna-bloodmoon: It crossed the sea and drifted ashore in [[Kitakami
basculegion-female: The souls of its comrades cloak its body and propel it effortlessly through even raging rapids.
oinkologne-female: This Pokémon sends a flowerlike scent wafting about. Well-developed muscles in its legs allow it to leap with no trouble at all.
squawkabilly-blue-plumage: The largest of their flocks can contain more than 50 individuals. They fly around towns and forests, searching for food and making a racket.
squawkabilly-yellow-plumage: These Squawkabilly are hotheaded, and their fighting style is vicious. They’ll leap within reach of their foes to engage in close combat.
squawkabilly-white-plumage: This Pokémon dislikes being alone. It has a strong sense of community and survives by cooperating with allies.
scovillain-mega: Mega Evolution has dialed up this Pokémon's spiciness. It swings its 'necktie' around to wallop its foes.
glimmora-mega: Glimmora's petals—now larger and separated from its main body—rotate around it to provide defense while scattering poisonous fragments.
baxcalibur-mega: Baxcalibur's dorsal blade has grown even more massive thanks to Mega Evolution. This Pokémon fires beams from the hilt at its solar plexus.
ogerpon-wellspring-mask: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.
ogerpon-hearthflame-mask: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.
ogerpon-cornerstone-mask: This form has excellent defenses, absorbing impacts solidly like the cornerstones that support houses.
rotom-heat: If the convection microwave oven is not working properly, then the Rotom inhabiting it will become lethargic.
rotom-wash: This Rotom has entered a washing machine. It nods with satisfaction after it floods the surrounding area.
rotom-frost: This Rotom has entered a refrigerator. It leaps around gleefully after it uses cold air to freeze the area around it.
rotom-fan: This Rotom has entered an electric fan. It smirks with satisfaction over a prank well pulled after it blows away everything around it.
rotom-mow: The lawn mower is one of the household appliances that led to the development of the Rotom Dex.
tornadus-therian: An example of what is known as a “form change,” though I suspect this strange avian guise to be Tornadus's true form. Tornadus has been sighted crossing the ocean while in this form.
calyrex-ice: According to lore, this Pokémon showed no mercy to those who got in its way, yet it would heal its opponents' wounds after battle.
calyrex-shadow: It's said that Calyrex and a Spectrier that had bonded with it ran all across the Galar region to bring green to the wastelands.
tauros-paldea-combat-breed: This Pokémon has a muscular body and excels at close-quarters combat. It uses its short horns to strike the opponent’s weak spots.
tauros-paldea-blaze-breed: When heated by fire energy, its horns can get hotter than . Those gored by them will suffer both wounds and burns.
tauros-paldea-aqua-breed: This Pokémon blasts water from holes on the tips of its horns—the high-pressure jets pierce right through Tauros’s enemies.
castform-sunny: Castform changes to this form when it basks in bright sunlight. When you touch its glowing skin, it feels all dried out!
castform-rainy: This is Castform's form when pelted by rain. In an experiment where it was placed in a shower, this Pokémon didn't change to this form.
castform-snowy: This is Castform's form when caught in a hailstorm. Its whole body is chilled, and its skin is partially frozen!
deoxys-attack: This Deoxys has transformed into its aggressive guise. It can fool enemies by altering its appearance.
deoxys-defense: When it changes form, an aurora appears. It absorbs attacks by altering its cellular structure.
deoxys-speed: A Pokémon that mutated from an extraterrestrial virus exposed to a laser beam. Its body is configured for superior agility and speed.
dialga-origin: Radiant light caused Dialga to take on a form bearing a striking resemblance to Arceus. Dialga now wields such colossal strength that one must conclude this is its true form.
palkia-origin: It soars across the sky in a form that greatly resembles the creator of all things. Perhaps this imitation of appearance is Palkia's strategy for gaining Arceus's powers.
giratina-origin: Giratina loses its legs upon changing into this form. I believe this Pokémon must hail from a world where the heavens and the earth are as one, though I have no way of proving it.
shaymin-sky: Upon taking in the scent of a particular rare flower, Shaymin is enveloped in light, and its tiny body transforms. I took a whiff of the flower myself, but alas, my body remained unchanged.
basculin-blue-striped: These Pokémon form schools with others of the same color. The greatest foe of a school of blue Basculin is a school of red Basculin.
basculin-white-striped: They live in frigid seas, forming schools and sharing food among themselves. When the time comes, they return to the river they were born in.
darmanitan-zen: Through meditation, it calms its raging spirit and hones its psychic powers.
darmanitan-galar-standard: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.
darmanitan-galar-zen: Anger has reignited its atrophied flame sac. This Pokémon spews fire everywhere as it rampages indiscriminately.
thundurus-therian: Quick as a flash, it materializes out of nowhere. It pulverizes foes into nothingness with showers of devastatingly powerful lightning bolts launched from the string of orbs on its tail.
landorus-therian: Landorus soars through the sky in this form, bestowing plentiful harvests upon the land and earning the people's reverence. It can traverse the whole of Hisui in a mere couple of hours.
kyurem-white: The sameness of Reshiram's and Kyurem's genes allowed Kyurem to absorb Reshiram. Kyurem can now use the power of both fire and ice.
kyurem-black: It's said that this Pokémon battles in order to protect the ideal world that will exist in the future for people and Pokémon.
keldeo-resolute: Keldeo has strengthened its resolve for battle, filling its body with power and changing its form.
meloetta-pirouette: Now that it has reclaimed a long-forgotten song, Meloetta captivates everyone with its enchanting dance steps.
aegislash-blade: This stance is dedicated to offense. It can cleave any opponent with the strength and weight of its steel blade.
pumpkaboo-small: When taking spirits to the afterlife, small Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of children to those of adults.
pumpkaboo-large: When taking spirits to the afterlife, large Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of adults to those of children.
pumpkaboo-super: Supersized Pumpkaboo are very partial to the spirits of people who were of similarly superior proportions.
gourgeist-small: Small Gourgeist pretend to be children to fool adults. Anyone who falls for the act gets carried away to the hereafter.
gourgeist-large: Large Gourgeist put on the guise of adults, taking the hands of children to lead them to the afterlife.
gourgeist-super: Supersized Gourgeist aren't picky. They will forcefully drag anyone off to the afterlife.
zygarde-10: This is Zygarde when about 10% of its pieces have been assembled. It leaps at its opponent's chest and sinks its sharp fangs into them.
zygarde-complete: Born when all of Zygarde's cells have been gathered together, it uses force to neutralize those who harm the ecosystem.
hoopa-unbound: This is Hoopa's true form, in which it has regained its sealed powers. The hole in its chest is said to connect to another dimension.
oricorio-pom-pom: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.
oricorio-pau: The airy dance of these Oricorio is popular as a means of maintaining good health, but the Oricorio themselves are hard to find in Paldea.
oricorio-sensu: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.
lycanroc-midnight: This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.
lycanroc-dusk: Normally this Pokémon doesn't even bark, but once it enters battle, it will relentlessly drive the opponent into a corner.
wishiwashi-school: On their own, they're very weak. But when Wishiwashi pool their power together in a school, they become a demon of the sea.
minior-red: Minior that fall to the ground try to return to space, but their wish will not come true—all they can do is fade away.
mimikyu-busted: Even as it struggles beneath the cloth to repair the broken neck of its disguise, Mimikyu keeps up its impersonation of Pikachu.
necrozma-dusk: When it dominates Solgaleo, it takes on this form. It's a vicious Pokémon, mangling prey with its many claws—including those on its back.
necrozma-dawn: Necrozma has subjugated Lunala entirely, forcing the unfortunate to emit its light energy for Necrozma to consume.
necrozma-ultra: The light pouring out from all over its body affects living things and nature, impacting them in various ways.
magearna-original: This Magearna looks as it did at the time of its invention. Its magnificent ornamentation delighted the king's daughter.
cramorant-gulping: Cramorant is constantly on the hunt for food along riverbanks. It loves Arrokuda—when it finds them, it swallows them whole without a thought.
cramorant-gorging: Its throat is clearly not wide enough to swallow a Pikachu, and yet Cramorant makes no move to release its catch.
toxtricity-low-key: Many youths admire the way this Pokémon listlessly picks fights and keeps its cool no matter what opponent it faces.
toxtricity-amped-gmax: Its excessive electric energy is its weapon. This Pokémon can build up more electricity than any thundercloud.
eiscue-noice: The ice that was covering this Eiscue’s face has shattered. Eiscue emits cold air from the tip of its hair to protect its head, which is sensitive to heat.
morpeko-hangry: Even after using up all its dark energy, this violent ruffian will keep running riot until its hunger is appeased.
zacian-crowned: Now armed with a weapon it used in ancient times, this Pokémon needs only a single strike to fell even Gigantamax Pokémon.
zamazenta-crowned: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.
eternatus-eternamax: As a result of Rose's meddling, Eternatus absorbed all the energy in the Galar region. It's now in a state of power overload.
urshifu-rapid-strike: It's believed that this Pokémon modeled its fighting style on the flow of a river—sometimes rapid, sometimes calm.
urshifu-single-strike-gmax: People call it the embodiment of rage. It's said that this Pokémon's terrifying expression and shout will rid the world of malevolence.
urshifu-rapid-strike-gmax: As it waits for the right moment to unleash its Gigantamax power, this Pokémon maintains a perfect one-legged stance. It won't even twitch.
enamorus-therian: A different guise from its feminine humanoid form. From the clouds, it descends upon those who treat any form of life with disrespect and metes out wrathful, ruthless punishment.
palafin-hero: This Pokémon's ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.
tatsugiri-droopy: This species' differing colors and patterns are apparently the result of Tatsugiri changing itself to suit the preferences of the prey it lures in.
tatsugiri-stretchy: Bird Pokémon are its main prey. This Pokémon knows it's weak, so it hunts with a partner.
dudunsparce-three-segment: The gentle Dudunsparce will put Pokémon that wander into its nest onto its back and carry them to the entrance.
gimmighoul-roaming: This Pokémon was born from passion that seeped into a coin. It wanders, apparently seeking to return to the treasure chest it once inhabited.
terapagos-terastal: Upon sensing danger, it prepares itself for battle by creating a sturdy shell of crystallized Terastal energy.
terapagos-stellar: In this form, Terapagos resembles the world as the ancients saw it, and its Terastal energy is abnormally amplified.
raticate-alola: It commands a nest of Rattata. Different nests don't get along, whipping up severe fights over feeding grounds.
mr-mime-galar: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.
wormadam-sandy: Its earthen skin is reasonably hard—it has no problem repelling a Starly's pecking, at least.
wormadam-trash: Its body, composed of refuse, blends in to the scenery so much as to be inconspicuous. This seems to be the perfect way for the Pokémon to evade the detection of predators.
maushold-family-of-three: They build huge nests with many rooms that are used for different purposes, such as eating and sleeping.
goodra: It loves the rain. This mellow Pokémon can be seen walking around on the plains and in the mountains on rainy days.
decidueye: It cunningly conceals arrow quills in its wings. It launches these quills with such precision that they can pierce a pebble at over a hundred yards.
charizard: If Charizard becomes truly angered, the flame at the tip of its tail burns in a light blue shade.
vulpix: If it is attacked by an enemy that is stronger than itself, it feigns injury to fool the enemy and escapes.
growlithe: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.
tauros: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.
rotom: Its electricity-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.
venusaur: While it basks in the sun, it can convert the light into energy. As a result, it is more powerful in the summertime.
blastoise: It deliberately increases its body weight so it can withstand the recoil of the water jets it fires.
butterfree: In battle, it flaps its wings at great speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.
beedrill: They may appear in a swarm. Beedrill flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.
pidgeot: It flies over water as it hunts, skimming along the surface to catch Magikarp and other such prey.
rattata: It's cautious in the extreme, and its hardy vitality lets it live in any kind of environment.
raticate: It whittles down its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder block walls.
raichu: Its tail discharges electricity into the ground, protecting it from getting shocked.
sandshrew: It digs deep burrows to live in. When in danger, it rolls up its body to withstand attacks.
sandslash: It is adept at attacking with the spines on its back and its sharp claws while quickly scurrying about.
clefable: Said to live in quiet, remote mountains, this type of fairy has a strong aversion to being seen.
ninetales: Some legends claim that each of its nine tails has its own unique type of special mystical power.
diglett: It lives about one yard underground, where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.
dugtrio: Its three heads bob separately up and down to loosen the soil nearby, making it easier for it to burrow.
meowth: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.
persian: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.
arcanine: An ancient picture scroll shows that people were captivated by its movement as it ran through prairies.
alakazam: It has an incredibly high level of intelligence. Some say that Alakazam remembers everything that ever happens to it, from birth till death.
machamp: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.
victreebel: It lures prey into its mouth with a nectar-like aroma. The helpless prey is melted with a dissolving fluid.
geodude: At rest, it looks just like a rock. Carelessly stepping on it will make it swing its fists angrily.
graveler: A slow walker, it rolls to move. It pays no attention to any object that happens to be in its path.
golem: It is enclosed in a hard shell that is as rugged as slabs of rock. It sheds skin once a year to grow larger.
ponyta: It can't run properly when it's newly born. As it races around with others of its kind, its legs grow stronger.
rapidash: This Pokémon can be seen galloping through fields at speeds of up to , its fiery mane fluttering in the wind.
slowpoke: It is incredibly slow and dopey. It takes five seconds for it to feel pain when under attack.
slowbro: When a Slowpoke went hunting in the sea, its tail was bitten by a Shellder. That made it evolve into Slowbro.
farfetchd: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.
grimer: Born from sludge, these Pokémon now gather in polluted places and increase the bacteria in their bodies.
muk: It’s thickly covered with a filthy, vile sludge. It is so toxic, even its footprints contain poison.
gengar: To steal the life of its target, it slips into the prey’s shadow and silently waits for an opportunity.
kingler: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.
voltorb: It rolls to move. If the ground is uneven, a sudden jolt from hitting a bump can cause it to explode.
electrode: The more energy it charges up, the faster it gets. But this also makes it more likely to explode.
exeggutor: It is called the Walking Jungle. Each of the nuts has a face and a will of its own.
marowak: This Pokémon overcame its sorrow to evolve a sturdy new body. Marowak faces its opponents bravely, using a bone as a weapon.
weezing: Top-grade perfume is made using its internal poison gases by diluting them to the highest level.
kangaskhan: Although it's carrying its baby in a pouch on its belly, Kangaskhan is swift on its feet. It intimidates its opponents with quick jabs.
starmie: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.
mr-mime: The broadness of its hands may be no coincidence—many scientists believe its palms became enlarged specifically for pantomiming.
pinsir: These Pokémon judge one another based on pincers. Thicker, more impressive pincers make for more popularity with the opposite gender.
gyarados: Once it appears, it goes on a rampage. It remains enraged until it demolishes everything around it.
lapras: It ferries people across the sea on its back. It may sing an enchanting cry if it is in a good mood.
eevee: Its ability to evolve into many forms allows it to adapt smoothly and perfectly to any environment.
aerodactyl: This is a ferocious Pokémon from ancient times. Apparently even modern technology is incapable of producing a perfectly restored specimen.
snorlax: This gluttonous Pokémon eats constantly, apart from when it's asleep. It devours nearly 900 pounds of food per day.
articuno: It's said that this Pokémon's beautiful blue wings are made of ice. Articuno flies over snowy mountains, its long tail fluttering along behind it.
zapdos: This Pokémon has complete control over electricity. There are tales of Zapdos nesting in the dark depths of pitch-black thunderclouds.
moltres: It's one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.
dragonite: It is said that somewhere in the ocean lies an island where these gather. Only they live there.
mewtwo: Created from the DNA of Mew, this Pokémon is a dangerous combination of overwhelming power and a savage heart.
meganium: Anyone who stands beside it becomes refreshed, just as if they were soaking in the soothing atmosphere of a forest.
typhlosion: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur to cause huge explosions.
feraligatr: When it bites with its massive and powerful jaws, it shakes its head and savagely tears its victim up.
ampharos: The bright light on its tail can be seen far away. It has been treasured since ancient times as a beacon.
wooper: A transparent mucous membrane covers its body. Touching it bare-handed will cause a tingling numbness.
slowking: When its head was bitten, toxins entered Slowpoke’s head and unlocked an extraordinary power.
steelix: It is said that if an Onix lives for over 100 years, its composition changes to become diamond-like.
qwilfish: Be cautious if this Pokémon starts sucking in water—it will soon attack by scattering the toxic spikes that grow all over its body.
scizor: This Pokémon’s pincers, which contain steel, can crush any hard object they get ahold of into bits.
heracross: It loves sweet nectar. To keep all the nectar to itself, it hurls rivals away with its prized horn.
sneasel: This cunning Pokémon hides under the cover of darkness, waiting to attack its prey.
corsola: It will regrow any branches that break off its head. People keep particularly beautiful Corsola branches as charms to promote safe childbirth.
skarmory: Its sturdy, heavy-looking iron body is actually thin and light, so it can fly at speeds over 180 mph.
houndoom: If you are burned by the flames it shoots from its mouth, the pain will never go away.
tyranitar: Extremely strong, it can change the landscape. It is so insolent that it doesn't care about others.
sceptile: It agilely flits through the jungle and uses the sharp-edged leaves on its arms to cut down its prey.
blaziken: When facing a tough foe, it looses flames from its wrists. Its powerful legs let it jump clear over buildings.
swampert: Its arms are hard as rock. With one swing, it can break an enormous boulder into pieces.
zigzagoon: It marks its territory by rubbing its bristly fur on trees. This variety of Zigzagoon is friendlier and calmer than the kind native to Galar.
linoone: Its fur is strong and supple. Shaving brushes made with shed Linoone hairs are highly prized.
gardevoir: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.
sableye: It dwells in the darkness of caves. It uses its sharp claws to dig up gems to nourish itself.
mawile: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.
aggron: Aggron has a horn sharp enough to perforate thick iron sheets. It brings down its opponents by ramming into them horn first.
medicham: Through yoga training, it gained the psychic power to predict its foe’s next move.
manectric: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.
sharpedo: As soon as it catches the scent of its prey, Sharpedo will jet seawater from its backside, hurtling toward the target to attack at .
camerupt: It lives in the crater of a volcano. It is well known that the humps on its back erupt every 10 years.
altaria: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.
castform: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.
banette: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.
chimecho: It uses the sucker on its head to hang from a tree or from eaves. It can produce seven different tones.
absol: Swift as the wind, Absol races through fields and mountains. Its curved, bow-like horn is acutely sensitive to the warning signs of natural disasters.
glalie: Its prey is instantaneously frozen stiff by the cold air it exhales from its huge mouth. While they’re in that frozen state, it gobbles them up.
salamence: It flies around on its wings, which have grown in at last. In its happiness, it gushes hot flames, burning up everything it passes over.
metagross: It folds its four legs when flying. Its four brains are said to be superior to a supercomputer.
latias: It communicates its feelings using telepathy. To make itself invisible, it covers its body in down that refracts light.
latios: It understands human speech and is highly intelligent. It is a tender Pokémon that dislikes fighting.
kyogre: In myths, this Pokémon expanded the seas with torrential rains and great tsunamis. It battled ferociously against Groudon.
groudon: Groudon is said to have expanded the reach of dry land by evaporating water with raging heat. It battled ferociously against Kyogre.
rayquaza: Rayquaza cannot be seen from the ground because it lives in the ozone layer, far above the clouds.
deoxys: An alien virus that fell to earth on a meteorite underwent a DNA mutation to become this Pokémon.
staraptor: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.
wormadam: Its appearance changes depending on where it evolved. The materials on hand become a part of its body.
lopunny: Lopunny is constantly monitoring its surroundings. If dangers approaches, this Pokémon responds with superdestructive kicks.
garchomp: It is said that when one runs at high speed, its wings create blades of wind that can fell nearby trees.
lucario: It’s said that no foe can remain invisible to Lucario, since it can detect auras—even those of foes it could not otherwise see.
abomasnow: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.
gallade: When trying to protect someone, it extends its elbows as if they were swords and fights savagely.
froslass: When it finds humans or Pokémon it likes, it freezes them and takes them to its chilly den, where they become decorations.
dialga: This Pokémon is revered as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Dialga was what caused the vast river of time to begin flowing in our world.
palkia: This Pokémon is feared as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Palkia was what caused the walls of our world to disappear, creating a sky that spans for infinity.
heatran: Boiling, magma-like blood circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.
giratina: A Pokémon that is said to live in a world on the reverse side of ours. It appears in an ancient cemetery.
darkrai: It can lull people into a deep sleep and make them dream. It is active on nights with a new moon.
shaymin: It can dissolve toxins in the air to instantly transform ruined land into a lush field of flowers.
emboar: The flames blazing deep inside Emboar’s chest erupt vigorously from its chin, forming a beard of fire.
samurott: A master of a sword-drawing art called iaijutsu, Samurott settles its battles with a single swing of either large sword sheathed in its foreleg armor.
excadrill: Forming a drill with its steel claws and head, it can bore through a steel plate, no matter how thick it is.
audino: Audino's sense of hearing is superb. Not even a pebble rolling along over a mile away will escape Audino's ears.
scolipede: Scolipede latches on to its prey with the claws on its neck before slamming them into the ground and jabbing them with its claw's toxic spikes.
lilligant: The fragrance of the garland on its head has a relaxing effect, but taking care of it is very difficult.
basculin: Red and blue Basculin usually do not get along, but sometimes members of one school mingle with the other's school.
darumaka: It derives its power from fire burning inside its body. If the fire dwindles, this Pokémon will immediately fall asleep.
darmanitan: Its internal fire burns at , making enough power that it can destroy a dump truck with one punch.
scrafty: Groups of them beat up anything that enters their territory. Each can spit acidic liquid from its mouth.
yamask: It wanders through ruins by night, carrying a mask that's said to have been the face it had when it was still human.
garbodor: This Pokémon eats trash, which turns into poison inside its body. The main component of the poison depends on what sort of trash was eaten.
zorua: It changes into the forms of others to surprise them. Apparently, it often transforms into a silent child.
zoroark: Stories say those who tried to catch Zoroark were trapped in an illusion and punished.
eelektross: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.
chandelure: The spirits burned up in its ominous flame lose their way and wander this world forever.
stunfisk: Thanks to bacteria that lived in the mud flats with it, this Pokémon developed the organs it uses to generate electricity.
golurk: When the seal on its chest is removed, it rages indiscriminately. During such rampages, it can turn a whole town into a mountain of rubble.
braviary: The more scars they have, the more respect these brave soldiers of the sky get from their peers.
tornadus: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.
thundurus: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.
landorus: From the forces of lightning and wind, it creates energy to give nutrients to the soil and make the land abundant.
kyurem: Dwelling within it is a power even greater than that of Reshiram or Zekrom, but the extreme cold keeps that power bound.
keldeo: When it is resolute, its body fills with power and it becomes swifter. Its jumps are then too fast to follow.
meloetta: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.
chesnaught: When its allies are in trouble, Chesnaught creates a shield with its arms and uses its own body to block attacks.
delphox: It leaves burn marks in the ground with the flame at the tip of its wand. Long ago, people used the shapes of these burn marks for divination.
greninja: The movement of air against the long tongue Greninja keeps wrapped around its neck allows it to sense enemies it can’t see.
pyroar: The females of a pride work together to bring down prey. It’s thanks to them that their pride doesn’t starve.
floette: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.
meowstic: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.
aegislash: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.
malamar: Its hypnotic powers are dangerously strong. Anyone who falls under Malamar’s control loses their memories surrounding the event.
barbaracle: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.
dragalge: Using a liquid poison, Dragalge indiscriminately attacks anything that wanders into its territory. This poison can corrode the undersides of boats.
hawlucha: Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.
sliggoo: The swirly protrusion on its back is filled with all its vital organs, such as its brain and heart.
pumpkaboo: It is said to carry wandering spirits to the place where they belong so they can move on.
gourgeist: It enwraps its prey in its hairlike arms. It sings joyfully as it observes the suffering of its prey.
avalugg: As Avalugg walks along with Bergmite on its back, it comes across pods of Cetitan. It lets them pass to avoid conflict.
zygarde: It's hypothesized that it's monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.
diancie: Diancie is a spontaneous mutation of Carbink. Its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.
hoopa: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.
crabominable: The detached pincers of these Pokémon are delicious. Some Trainers bring Lechonk into the mountains just to search for them.
golisopod: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.
drampa: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.
necrozma: It survives by absorbing light. After a long time spent slumbering underground, impurities accumulated within it, causing its body to darken.
magearna: This Pokémon was built by a scientist roughly 500 years ago. The part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.
zeraora: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.
melmetal: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.
rillaboom: Though it has a calm disposition, it won’t tolerate those who drum up discord. It strictly disciplines offenders until they learn their lesson.
cinderace: Although Cinderace loses its cool easily, it will battle flawlessly for a Trainer it trusts.
inteleon: It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.
corviknight: Corviknight can’t serve as a taxi service in Paldea because the Pokémon’s Tinkatons will attack it while it flies, endangering the customer.
orbeetle: It's famous for its high level of intelligence, and the large size of its brain is proof that it also possesses immense psychic power.
drednaw: Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.
coalossal: To intimidate its opponents, Coalossal will vigorously shake its body, scattering coal from its smoldering back.
flapple: It flutters around while seeking an opening in the opponent’s guard, then attacks by spitting acidic liquid strong enough to melt metal.
appletun: It feeds mainly on sweet apples. It will also eat small bug Pokémon that are attracted by its sweet nectar.
sandaconda: It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.
cramorant: Cramorant instinctively swallow everything whole. They're at their most formidable when they're struggling to swallow overly large prey.
centiskorch: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about . It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.
hatterene: Hatterene knocks out those that intrude in its home forest by blasting them with a beam, then slashing with claws enhanced by psychic power.
grimmsnarl: This Pokémon has complete control over its hair. Grimmsnarl normally keeps its hair wrapped around its body to support its muscles.
falinks: The brass, which is the one that stands at the front and issues orders, is the strongest and smartest of the six.
copperajah: This Pokémon was brought to Paldea long ago by people from a faraway land. It’s so strong that it can easily pull an airplane.
duraludon: Duraludon’s body is comprised of a special metal that’s lightweight and scratch resistant. It’s also smooth, as though it was given a mirror finish.
eternatus: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.
zarude: Within dense forests, this Pokémon lives in a pack with others of its kind. It's incredibly aggressive, and the other Pokémon of the forest fear it.
calyrex: Calyrex is a merciful Pokémon, capable of providing healing and blessings. It reigned over the Galar region in times of yore.
ursaluna: I believe it was Hisui's swampy terrain that gave Ursaluna its burly physique and newfound capacity to manipulate peat at will.
basculegion: Clads itself in the souls of comrades that perished before fulfilling their goals of journeying upstream. No other species throughout all Hisui's rivers is Basculegion's equal.
scovillain: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.
glimmora: When this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.
baxcalibur: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.
tatsugiri-curly-mega: Tatsugiri's brain has been invigorated by Mega Evolution, making it even wilier. It can create and command copies of itself.
tatsugiri-droopy-mega: It solidifies the energy of Mega Evolution, building up an overflowing pile to launch as projectiles. These projectiles explode on contact.
tatsugiri-stretchy-mega: Using the energy of Mega Evolution, it creates a dish to ride upon, allowing it to move with total freedom-even through the air.
magearna-original-mega: A mechanism to remove Magearna's limitations has lain secretly within Magearna for 500 years. This mechanism is triggered by a Mega Stone.
